# US Stock Watcher Next-Session Forecast — Tuesday 7/7

> **[Tuesday 7/7 session outlook] Bullish lean — Strong expansion / bull · composite +40 · confidence 49%**

> **🔮 Next-session forecast** · The next U.S. regular session is Tuesday 7/7 (opens 9:30 AM ET / 22:30 KST 7/7).

_Data as of: 2026-07-06T22:39:20 · data quality: fresh_

## Executive Summary

With the market closed and the next U.S. regular session set for Tuesday 7/7 (opening 9:30 AM ET / 22:30 KST 7/7), this brief looks ahead to that session rather than recapping the prior close. The overall market-state score stands at +40 out of 100, placing conditions in "Strong expansion / bull" territory, with confidence at 49% based on four measured components. Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning remain unmeasured and have been reweighted out of the composite, so the reading should be treated as directionally informative rather than exhaustive.

## Market Diagnosis

Heading into Tuesday's open, the major indices carry constructive momentum from their most recent settings: the S&P 500 closed at 7,537.43, up 0.72% on the day; the Nasdaq-100 finished at 29,697.87, up a stronger 1.26%; the Dow Jones Industrial Average settled at 53,055.91, up a more modest 0.29%; and the Russell 2000 ended at 3,009.54, up 0.45%.

Beneath the index-level moves, the underlying drivers are mixed. Volatility is supportive of risk assets, and the yield curve is likewise supportive, but index trend and the U.S. dollar are both acting as headwinds — a combination that tempers an otherwise bullish backdrop.

On breadth and concentration, the composite market-state score of +40/100 continues to point to "Strong expansion / bull" conditions, with confidence held at 49% across the four components that are currently measured, while Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning remain excluded from the calculation.

Sector rotation into the new session favors Financials, Health Care, and Industrials as the leadership group, while Communication Services, Technology, and Energy lag.

In rates, currencies, and commodities, the 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.49%, the dollar index (DXY) is essentially flat at 100.86 (-0.00% 1d), WTI crude is little changed at 68.66 (-0.04% 1d), and gold has advanced to 4,176.60, up 1.55% on the day.

## Outlook

**Short term.** Framed specifically for the Tuesday 7/7 session, the near-term bias is constructive, consistent with the "Strong expansion / bull" market state and its 49% confidence level. This view would flip should the overall composite score cross back below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term stance carries the same constructive bias, again anchored to the "Strong expansion / bull" reading at 49% confidence, with a break below zero on the overall score serving as the trigger for reassessment.

**Medium-to-long term.** Extending further out, the medium-to-long-term view remains constructive on identical grounds — the "Strong expansion / bull" state at 49% confidence — and would likewise reverse if the overall score turns negative.

## Top News

Coverage feeding into this outlook includes: "US Stock Market Today: S&P 500 Futures Rise As Inflation Jitters Temporarily Ease" (Yahoo Finance); "These 3 Stocks Trounced the S&P 500 in the First Half of 2026 -- and Wall Street Thinks 1 of Them Could Soar Even More" (The Motley Fool); "Asian Stocks Set to Gain After Tech-Powered Rally: Markets Wrap" (Bloomberg.com); "Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Rises at Start of Busy Week for Tech Stocks — Live Updates" (WSJ); "Stock Market Today, July 6: Semiconductor Momentum Lifts Nasdaq as Dow Closes Above 53,000" (The Motley Fool); and "S&P 500, Nasdaq close sharply higher as Broadcom rallies; TSX edges lower" (The Globe and Mail).
